common.footerDescription: Lzecher is a platform for organizing communal Torah learning in memory of loved ones.
common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

landing.heroTitle: Honor Their Memory Through Torah Learning
landing.heroDescription: Create a memorial page, choose learning tracks, and invite family and friends to participate in communal Torah study dedicated to the neshama of your loved one.
landing.featuresSubtitle: A complete platform for organizing and tracking communal Torah learning in memory of a loved one.
landing.feature_multiTrack_desc: Choose from Mishnayos, Tehillim, Shnayim Mikra, 613 Mitzvot, and more to create a comprehensive memorial learning program.
landing.feature_multiLanguage_desc: Available in English and Hebrew so communities around the world can participate with ease.
landing.feature_communal_desc: Invite family, friends, and community members to claim and complete portions together.
landing.feature_notifications_desc: Stay updated with email notifications when portions are claimed and completed.
landing.feature_shareLinks_desc: Share your memorial page with a simple link via email, WhatsApp, or any messaging platform.
landing.feature_moderated_desc: All memorial pages are reviewed to ensure respectful and appropriate content.
landing.tracksSubtitle: Choose one or more tracks for your memorial learning project.
landing.track_mishnayos_desc: Complete the entire Shisha Sidrei Mishna, divided into individual masechtos for participants to claim.
landing.track_tehillim_desc: Recite the entire Sefer Tehillim, divided by daily portions or individual chapters.
landing.track_shnayimMikra_desc: Complete the weekly parsha with Shnayim Mikra v'Echad Targum for each parsha of the year.
landing.track_mitzvot_desc: Study each of the 613 mitzvot, divided among participants for in-depth learning.
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: Setting up a memorial learning project takes just a few minutes.
landing.step_create_desc: Enter the niftar's details, write a short biography, and select which learning tracks to include.
landing.step_share_desc: Send the memorial link to family, friends, and community members via email or messaging apps.
landing.step_claim_desc: Participants choose which portions they would like to learn and claim them with their name.
landing.step_learn_desc: Each participant learns their portion and marks it complete, tracking progress toward the shared goal.
landing.ctaDescription: Honor the memory of your loved one by uniting family and friends in Torah learning. It only takes a few minutes to set up.

about.title: About Lzecher
about.subtitle: Honoring memory through the timeless power of Torah learning.
about.mission: Lzecher was created with a simple but profound mission: to make it easy for families and communities to organize Torah learning in memory of a loved one. When someone passes away, one of the greatest gifts we can give their neshama is the merit of Torah study dedicated in their memory.
about.vision: We believe that technology can serve the highest purposes. By providing a modern, intuitive platform, we remove the logistical barriers that often make it difficult to coordinate communal learning. No more spreadsheets, no more phone calls — just a clean, simple experience that lets people focus on what matters most: the learning itself.
about.howItHelps: With Lzecher, a family member can set up a memorial page in minutes, choose from multiple learning tracks — Mishnayos, Tehillim, Shnayim Mikra, and the 613 Mitzvot — and share a link with family, friends, and community members around the world. Participants claim their portions and mark them complete, with everyone able to see the collective progress in real time.
about.community: Lzecher is built for Klal Yisrael. It is free to use and open to all who wish to participate in this beautiful mitzvah. We are committed to maintaining the highest standards of respect, accuracy, and halachic integrity in everything we do.
about.dedication: May the Torah learned through this platform be a zechus for all the neshamot in whose memory it is dedicated, and may their families find comfort in the unity and devotion of those who learn on their behalf.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Lzecher ("we", "our", or "us") respects your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information when you use our platform.
privacy.collection_content: We collect the following types of information: your name and email address when you create an account; memorial details you provide (names, biographies, dates); your participation activity such as claimed and completed portions; and basic usage data to improve the platform experience.
privacy.use_content: We use your information to operate and improve the Lzecher platform, display memorial pages and track learning progress, send notifications about portions you have claimed or projects you are involved in, and communicate important updates about the service.
privacy.sharing_content: We do not sell your personal information. Your name may be displayed on memorial pages when you claim or complete a portion. We may share information with service providers who help us operate the platform, such as hosting and email delivery services.
privacy.security_content: We take reasonable measures to protect your personal information from unauthorized access, alteration, or destruction. However, no method of transmission over the internet is completely secure.
privacy.rights_content: You may access, update, or delete your personal information at any time by contacting us. You may also request a copy of the data we hold about you.
privacy.contact_content: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at support@lzecher.com.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to Lzecher. By using our platform, you agree to these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully.
terms.eligibility_content: You must be at least 13 years of age to use Lzecher. By creating an account, you confirm that you meet this requirement.
terms.accounts_content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account and for all activity that occurs under your account. You must provide accurate information when creating a memorial page.
terms.content_content: You retain ownership of the content you submit, including biographies and memorial details. By posting content on Lzecher, you grant us a license to display it on the platform. All content must be respectful, accurate, and appropriate. We reserve the right to remove content that violates these standards.
terms.conduct_content: You agree not to misuse the platform, submit false or misleading information, attempt to disrupt or interfere with the service, or use the platform for any purpose other than its intended use of organizing Torah learning.
terms.termination_content: We may suspend or terminate your access to the platform at any time if you violate these terms or engage in behavior that is harmful to other users or the service.
terms.liability_content: Lzecher is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of the platform.
terms.changes_content: We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. We will notify users of significant changes via email or a notice on the platform.
terms.contact_content: If you have questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at support@lzecher.com.

halachicGuidance.title: Halachic Guidance
halachicGuidance.subtitle: Torah perspectives on learning as a merit for the departed.
halachicGuidance.intro: The concept of learning Torah l'iluy nishmas (for the elevation of the soul) is deeply rooted in Jewish tradition. Below is a brief overview of the halachic basis for this practice.
halachicGuidance.mishnayos_content: The widespread custom of learning Mishnayos for the niftar is based on the teaching that the letters of "Mishna" (משנה) are the same as "Neshama" (נשמה). The study of Mishna is considered especially beneficial for the soul. It is customary to complete all of Shisha Sidrei Mishna within the first year after passing, and again on each yahrzeit.
halachicGuidance.tehillim_content: Reciting Tehillim (Psalms) has been a universal Jewish practice for centuries, both as a merit for the living and for the departed. King David composed the Psalms with divine inspiration, and their recitation carries profound spiritual power.
halachicGuidance.shnayimMikra_content: The obligation to read the weekly Torah portion twice in the original text and once in Aramaic translation (or with Rashi's commentary) is a well-established halacha. Dedicating this learning as a merit for a loved one adds a dimension of chessed shel emes.
halachicGuidance.mitzvot_content: Studying the 613 mitzvot provides a comprehensive overview of the Torah's commandments. According to many poskim, Torah study in any form generates merit for the neshama when dedicated in their memory.
halachicGuidance.general_content: The merit of communal Torah learning is considered especially powerful. When multiple people join together to learn, the collective zechus is greater than the sum of its parts. Lzecher facilitates this by making it simple to divide and coordinate learning among many participants.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: We'd love to hear from you. Whether you have a question, feedback, or need assistance, feel free to reach out.
